published_prior_art.cite: ManskiTamer2002 + BeresteanuMolchanovMolinari2011/2012: generic interval/random-set selection machinery covers the algebraic envelope pattern, but not the text-treatment selector semantics.

BeresteanuMolchanovMolinari2012: random-set partial identification via selections/Aumann expectations is the closest general result; the representation-distortion target is new but algebraically close.

ImaiNakamura2024TextTreatments + CorcollAndreuVlontzosORiordanGilliganLee2026: exact/causal-latent representation endpoints are published; bounded-distortion validity boundary is not, but reduces to interval singleton plus calibration algebra.
ManskiTamer2002 + BeresteanuMolchanovMolinari2012: singletonness and endpoint equality are routine interval/envelope algebra.

novelty_flags.label: proposal
novelty_flags.one_line: tier=field below novelty_target=flagship; current kernel is a useful GenAI-treatment application of random-set/interval selector algebra, but needs a non-routine representation-learning-specific theorem beyond the BMM/Manski envelope pattern to meet the flagship floor.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Upgrade Conjecture 2 from endpoint algebra to a genuinely representation-specific result, e.g. characterize when empirical or model-certified distortion balls from GenAI embeddings imply testable fiber-invariance/calibration restrictions rather than assuming an abstract epsilon ball.

Add a theorem tying the envelope to an estimable robust sign decision with uniform inference or finite-sample auditing over a compact target class; this would separate the contribution from generic sharp-set algebra.

Include an explicit comparison theorem showing exactly how the proposed selector frontier is not a direct corollary of BMM random-set/Aumann expectation machinery, or concede that the novelty is application-level.

Quote the specific GenAI endpoint definition from ImaiNakamura2024TextTreatments in the Conjecture 2 novelty block so the negative/validity-boundary target is pinned to a published estimand rather than only a workflow description.

recommended_next_step: Revise around a stronger, representation-learning-specific theorem or lower the target from flagship to field.
